name: Wilbur Sweatman
abstract: Wilbur Coleman Sweatman (February 7, 1882 – March 9, 1961) was an American ragtime and dixieland jazz composer, bandleader and clarinetist. Sweatman was one of the first African-American musicians to have fans nationwide.

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: I was with Sweatman oh, about a week and a half. He gave me my two-week notice and fired me on account of that.

about_quotes.speaker: Sonny Greer
about_quotes.evidence: So he had this engagement at the Lafayette Theatre and so I got that gig for us. I got that for us, we played around New York, in New Jersey

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Whaley
about_quotes.evidence: he had a group and he wanted a piano player so they picked me and I went with him.

about_quotes.speaker: Dominic James "Nick" La Rocca
about_quotes.evidence: There was a few recordings by coloured bands. There was Jim Europe on Victor, there was Wilbur Sweatman on Columbia... But one's only got to listen to those and compare them with the original Dixieland Jazz Band to realise that the one is Ragtime and the other is Jazz.
